title: narcissus triandrus subsp. calathinus
intro: Narcissus triandrus subsp. calathinus, commonly known as the Triandrus daffodil or Bell-shaped narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Narcissus. This plant is known for its fragrant, bell-shaped flowers and is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Narcissus triandrus subsp. calathinus include its ability to aid in the relief of symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve the use of its bulbs, which can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
